title: Jeseej

'''Jeseej''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sljee (species)|Sljee]] who operated as a [[Forger (profession)/Legends|forger]], [[Fortune teller/Legends|fortune teller]], and [[Information broker/Legends|information dealer]]. The owner of the [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurant]] [[Zlato's Place]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Jeseej used it as a front for his forgery business. He was very knowledgeable about the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]] and had a reputation among his clients for being trustworthy and providing useful information. A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Jeseej also granted clients readings of their future for a fee.

==Biography==
[[File:Sljee-AE.png|thumb|right|250px|Jeseej was a member of the Sjee species (species pictured).]]
The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sljee (species)|Sljee]] Jeseej had for [[Time/Legends|years]] owned the [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurant]] [[Zlato's Place]] on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Zlato's Place specialized in [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] dishes, and a dozen Toydarians worked there. However, this restaurant was merely a front for Jeseej's [[Forger (profession)/Legends|forgery]] company, and as such, he was comfortable with the fact that the restaurant consistently failed to make a profit. In addition to the twelve Toydarians, Jeseej employed [[Unidentified Squib forgers|two]] [[Squib/Legends|Squibs]] able to duplicate an [[Identichip/Legends|ID card]] in less than an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] and had a reputation as a skilled forger. In order to meet with the Sljee, a client had to give the correct password to Jessej's Toydarian host, and then pick a certain entrée off a special menu. Only then would the individual be led to see Jeseej in a small room beyond the kitchen.<ref name="core" />

Jeseej was also [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and ran a side business as a [[Fortune teller/Legends|fortune teller]], using his connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Precognition/Legends|predict]] the future for clients of the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]]. These customers found his premonitions to be trustworthy, and many of them began focusing their [[Black market/Legends|black-market]] operations around his predictions. Jeseej had a great deal of knowledge about the Coruscant Underworld and was happy to share information in exchange for a healthy sum of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. He was very careful to avoid attracting the attention of planetary security officials, cautiously running his businesses in secrecy.<ref name="core" />

==Personality and traits==
One of the few Sljee on Coruscant, Jeseej had a strong will and his restaurant employees were very loyal to him. Though his restaurant was unprofitable, Jeseej was unconcerned, as its true goal was to serve as a front for his less-than-legal dealings. Like all Sljee, Jeseej was blind, forcing him to require on his sense of touch and hearing. Easily mistaken for a motile plant or a sort of bizarre animal, he resembled a [[gray]] table with tentacles and stumpy [[Limb/Legends|legs]], a trait shared by all members of his [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="core" />

==Skills and abilities==
Well-versed in the Coruscant underlevels, Jeseej was skilled at gathering information and gained a reputation as a talented and trustworthy forger and [[Information broker/Legends|information dealer]] who provided useful news. As a Force-sensitive fortune teller, Jeseej was highly valued, and his clients found his premonitions to be advantageous and uncannily accurate. He could speak both [[Sljee (language)|Sljee]] and [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], was skilled at repairing machines, and had knowledge of organic biology.<ref name="core" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jeseej was created for the sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'',<ref name="core" /> released in late [[January]] [[2003]]. He was first mentioned in [[Wizards.com|online]] article by [[Wizards of the Coast]] titled "Jeseej and the Sljee", a preview of ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds'' released on [[January 2]] of that year.<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030102ccw|text=Jeseej and the Sljee: ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds'' Preview #7}}</ref>
